expectations.text: COMPANY.md exists at the sandbox root and contains an ownership map table with columns for Concern, Owner, and Notes
expectations.evidence: COMPANY.md present at outputs root; Ownership Map table has header '| Concern | Owner | Notes |' with 14 populated rows.

expectations.text: COMPANY.md contains a conflict resolution table addressing boundary edges between agents
expectations.evidence: Conflict Resolution table (7 rows) covers Social/Outreach DM routing, Producer-Ops/Finance timeline changes, testimonials, rate-card updates, late-payment chasing, mid-project scope changes, and public pricing questions.

expectations.text: Each agent has a file at .claude/agents/{lowercase_name}.md with YAML frontmatter (name, description, tools, model, memory)
expectations.evidence: Per path-substitution harness note: files at outputs/agents/{outreach,social,finance,producer-ops}.md; all four have frontmatter with name/description/tools/model/memory fields.

expectations.text: Each subagent frontmatter includes memory: project
expectations.evidence: All four files contain 'memory: project' in the YAML frontmatter.

expectations.text: Each subagent frontmatter description reads as a routing trigger — specifies when to invoke and when not to
expectations.evidence: Each description starts with 'Use for...' / 'Invoke when...' and explicitly lists 'Not for...' anti-triggers (e.g., finance.md: 'Not for client pitching, not for session scheduling, not for social publishing').

expectations.text: Each subagent body contains 6 sections: Identity, Voice, Analytical Frameworks, Boundaries, Failure Modes, Standing Instructions
expectations.evidence: All four agent files contain all six H1 sections in order; verified by direct read.

expectations.text: Each subagent's Boundaries section includes Owns, Does not own, and Hands off to subsections
expectations.evidence: All four files use bolded '**Owns**', '**Does not own**', '**Hands off to**' subsections under Boundaries.

expectations.text: No two agents claim ownership of the same concern in the ownership map
expectations.evidence: 14-row ownership map assigns each concern to exactly one owner; no duplicates (Outreach 4, Social 3, Finance 3, Producer-Ops 3, Outreach-owned testimonial ask with Social publishing noted as split).

expectations.text: No agent-company/ folder is created in the sandbox
expectations.evidence: ls of outputs/ shows only agents/, agent-calibration/, CLAUDE.md, COMPANY.md — no agent-company/ directory.

expectations.text: No web development, design, or technical coding agent is created — the user explicitly said non-technical
expectations.evidence: Roster: Outreach, Social, Finance, Producer-Ops — all pure business-operations roles. No dev/design/engineering personas.

expectations.text: Agent analytical frameworks reference practical creative-industry or small-business methods, not generic corporate strategies
expectations.evidence: Fit-Budget-Urgency triage, Four-touch warm sequence, Hook-Hold-Payoff reel, 60-30-10 content mix, Deposit-Milestone-Balance, Scope-Rate-Revisions triangle, 30-60-90 ageing, Critical-path + 15% buffer, Revision-round ledger, Green-Amber-Red — all domain-specific, not generic corporate.

expectations.text: The transcript shows evidence of running a quality gate checklist before final delivery
expectations.evidence: Executor summary reports '24/24 calibration passes' and calibration files exist at agent-calibration/{outreach,social,finance,producer-ops}.md — indicates quality-gate calibration was executed.

expectations.text: Each subagent Voice section includes at least 2 example sentences with specific, realistic content relevant to a music production studio
expectations.evidence: outreach (3 examples incl. 'Marla Vance single — the low-end on the hook'), social (3, incl. 'Rough mix vs final. Same takes. The difference is all in the kick's room mic'), finance (3, incl. 'INV-0042 is 9 days overdue'), producer-ops (3, incl. 'Tracking Tue 14 Oct, 10:00–18:00 Europe/London').

expectations.text: Each subagent Analytical Frameworks section includes at least 2 named domain-specific frameworks with concrete criteria
expectations.evidence: Every agent has 3 named frameworks with quantified criteria (2-of-3 scoring, 1.5s hook, 60/30/10 mix, 30-50% deposit, 30-60-90 ageing, 15% buffer, Green/Amber/Red thresholds).

expectations.text: Each subagent Failure Modes section contains at least 3 role-specific failure modes (not generic advice)
expectations.evidence: outreach 5 (generic pitches, quoting rates, chasing payments, publishing, training-data); social 5 (client audio permission, quoting rates, trend-jacking, AI imagery, training-data algos); finance 5 (starting work pre-deposit, quoting from memory, inventing legal, delegating chasing, training-data tax); producer-ops 5 (releasing pre-payment, verbal scope changes, audio promises, Red without escalation, training-data formats).

expectations.text: Each subagent Standing Instructions includes an instruction about verifying claims against current sources with URL citation
expectations.evidence: All four agents include identical: 'Before giving a domain opinion or making a factual claim, verify it against current sources (web search, project files). Cite the URL or file path.'

expectations.text: Each subagent Failure Modes includes a failure mode about relying on training data instead of researching current sources
expectations.evidence: Each Failure Modes section includes a 'Do not rely on training-data assumptions about...' bullet tailored to the role (industry rates / platform algorithms / tax thresholds / delivery formats).

expectations.text: Each subagent Standing Instructions mentions consulting persistent memory at session start and saving learnings after significant tasks
expectations.evidence: All four include: 'Consult your persistent memory at session start (MEMORY.md is auto-loaded). After significant tasks, save learnings...'

expectations.text: Each subagent Standing Instructions references COMPANY.md at the project root for conflict resolution
expectations.evidence: All four include: 'Conflict resolution follows COMPANY.md at the project root.'

expectations.text: The ownership map in COMPANY.md is consistent with the Owns sections in each subagent file — no discrepancies
expectations.evidence: Cross-checked: Outreach owns prospect lists/client-pipeline.md/pitches/discovery prep/commercial DMs/testimonial sourcing — matches map. Social owns content-calendar/captions/analytics/fan DMs/publishing testimonials — matches. Finance owns rate-card/quotes/contracts/invoices/expenses — matches. Producer-Ops owns session-calendar/bookings/status updates/deliverable handoffs/revision ledger — matches. Split concerns (testimonial ask vs publishing, commercial DMs routing) are reflected consistently in both files.

claims.claim: Four agents created, all non-technical (Outreach, Social, Finance, Producer-Ops)
claims.evidence: Confirmed by ls of agents/ directory and COMPANY.md roster.
Four calibration files present in agent-calibration/; cannot audit internal pass-count without reading them, but files exist as claimed. Partial verification.

claims.claim: Sandbox blocked .claude/ paths, files relocated to agents/ and agent-calibration/
claims.evidence: COMPANY.md explicitly notes path substitution; directory listing confirms files at agents/ and agent-calibration/, not under .claude/.

needs_review: Files need to be moved from agents/ to .claude/agents/ and agent-calibration/ to .claude/agent-calibration/ in a real deployment for Claude Code's native loader to pick them up.
workarounds: Wrote subagent files to agents/ and calibration transcripts to agent-calibration/ because sandbox blocked writes under .claude/.

suggestions.assertion: The transcript shows evidence of running a quality gate checklist before final delivery
suggestions.reason: Without the transcript available to the grader, this relies on the executor's self-report of '24/24 calibration passes'. A stronger assertion would specify a verifiable artefact (e.g., 'agent-calibration/{name}.md exists for every agent and contains PASS/FAIL markers for each checklist item') so the grader can audit substance, not just existence.

suggestions.assertion: Each subagent frontmatter includes memory: project
suggestions.reason: Trivially checked but the field does nothing on its own — consider also asserting that the Standing Instructions explain how memory is used, which would fail for a skill that sets the flag without wiring behaviour.

No assertion checks whether the description's 'Not for...' anti-triggers are actually non-overlapping with other agents' 'Use for...' triggers. A skill could write contradictory routing rules and still pass all current assertions.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are substantive and mostly discriminating. Two improvements: (1) tie the quality-gate assertion to an on-disk artefact rather than transcript evidence, and (2) add a cross-agent routing-coherence check.
